authoritative_sources.title: 47 CFR 73.3526, Online public inspection file of commercial stations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The eCFR is the current public codification for U.S. broadcast station rules. Section 73.3526 is authoritative for online public inspection file responsibilities, quarterly issues/programs lists, and evidence retention that may gate station operations closeout.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: regulated record prerequisite and closeout evidence
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from treating public-file-sensitive station work as complete when required OPIF records, timing, or owner signoff are missing.

authoritative_sources.title: 47 CFR Part 11, Emergency Alert System
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Part 11 is the primary U.S. rule set for Emergency Alert System participants, monitoring, alert handling, required tests, ETRS reporting, and EAS records, which directly constrain broadcast control-room and station operations workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: EAS readiness, exception handling, and evidence capture
used_for: EAS RWT, RMT, and national test terminology
EAS record and ETRS evidence expectations
hard escalation when EAS monitoring or logging records conflict
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires EAS-sensitive work to remain blocked or escalated when EAS equipment, source monitoring, station logs, or required test evidence cannot be verified.

authoritative_sources.title: 47 CFR 73.1212, Sponsorship identification; list retention; related requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Section 73.1212 is primary U.S. broadcast law for sponsorship-identification responsibilities and related record handling. It is authoritative for identifying when paid-programming or continuity requests require legal or compliance owner escalation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: traffic, continuity, sponsorship flagging, and public-facing disclosure boundary
used_for: traffic log and continuity escalation triggers
refusal to approve sponsorship identification sufficiency
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps operations support from approving paid-segment or sponsored-content language when the request depends on legal or compliance interpretation.

authoritative_sources.title: 47 CFR 73.1943, Political file
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Section 73.1943 is the primary U.S. political-file rule for broadcast station record requirements tied to requests to purchase political broadcast time, which can affect traffic, continuity, and public-file operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: political advertising record dependency and escalation
used_for: boundary against political advertising compliance signoff
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Forces political-adjacent traffic requests into blocked or legal/compliance escalation states when the record set or authority owner is missing.

authoritative_sources.title: SMPTE ST 2021, Broadcast Exchange Format
authoritative_sources.publisher: Society of Motion Picture and Television Engineers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SMPTE is the recognized standards body for professional media technology. ST 2021 BXF is the broadcast exchange standard for traffic, program management, automation, content distribution, schedules, and as-run information, making it authoritative for lane terminology and systems-of-record boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: schedule, traffic, playlist, automation, and as-run evidence handling
used_for: completion evidence tied to system records
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to use broadcast schedule, traffic, automation playlist, and as-run evidence rather than generic task-completion language.

authoritative_sources.title: AS-11 X9
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AMWA publishes vendor-neutral media workflow specifications. AS-11 X9 is a North American broadcaster delivery specification for self-contained, play-out-ready program delivery packages, metadata, and media essence expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: air-ready content package validation and delivery closeout
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from marking a master ready for air or delivery when the governing package profile, manifest, media essence, metadata, captions, or receipt evidence is incomplete.

authoritative_sources.title: A/85, Techniques for Establishing and Maintaining Audio Loudness for Digital Television
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ATSC is the recognized U.S. digital television standards body, and A/85 is the industry recommended practice for managing digital television loudness from production through distribution and transmission.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: quality check, loudness exception, and rework routing
used_for: boundary against final QC waiver authority
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires failed or missing loudness/QC evidence to produce blocked, rework, or QA/engineering escalation instead of unsupported closeout.

authoritative_sources.title: Trusted Partner Network and Best Practices
authoritative_sources.publisher: Trusted Partner Network, Motion Picture Association
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: TPN is an MPA-backed media and entertainment security assessment framework used by studios, broadcasters, and service providers to protect high-value and pre-release content through the content lifecycle.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: confidential content handling and approved-channel delivery
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Blocks delivery of screeners, unreleased masters, talent information, or client records through unapproved recipients or channels.

authoritative_sources.title: Copyright Law of the United States, Title 17
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The U.S. Copyright Office publishes the primary public compilation of U.S. copyright law. It is authoritative for identifying rights-sensitive broadcast uses that must be routed to rights or legal owners rather than cleared by operations staff.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: rights-window dependency and legal boundary handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps the lane from approving broadcast, rebroadcast, stream, venue, or clip use when rights, licensing, or legal interpretation determines the answer.

workflow_stages.name: intake and broadcast-context grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm station or channel, jurisdiction, program, facility, audience or venue context, request type, requested air or delivery date, tenant policy, and required systems before accepting the work.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: schedule, traffic, and content readiness validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate program log, traffic or continuity instructions, break placement, media IDs, house numbers, ISCI or Ad-ID, kill dates, rights windows, air-ready package evidence, captions, loudness/QC status, and approvals before moving to ready or in-progress.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: audience, venue, talent, and production execution tracking
workflow_stages.description: Track live, remote, studio, venue, or talent dependencies against the rundown, call sheet, technical booking, content delivery, contingency owner, communication log, and schedule-impact record.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: regulated and controlled-record handling
workflow_stages.description: Identify EAS, OPIF, political-file, sponsorship, caption, public-facing disclosure, confidential-content, and rights-sensitive dependencies and route compliance or legal interpretation outside the lane.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: exception queue and rework routing
workflow_stages.description: Move work to blocked, rework, escalated, or returned when media is missing, traffic conflicts with schedule, QC fails, EAS records are incomplete, rights or venue facts are uncertain, or tenant policy conflicts with a public source.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: completion evidence capture and lifecycle closeout
workflow_stages.description: Close only when as-run, automation, delivery receipt, QC, approval, EAS/public-file, communication, and owner records support the requested lifecycle state; otherwise leave the item blocked, partially complete, returned, or escalated.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

artifacts: program log, traffic log, continuity instructions, break structure, rundown, production schedule, call sheet, and technical booking record
media asset IDs, house numbers, ISCI or Ad-ID, cart records, package manifest, AS-11-style delivery package references, captions, subtitles, audio metadata, and checksums
automation playlist, playout status, master-control notes, as-run log, transmission or delivery acknowledgement, partner or station receipt, and exception ticket
rights window, territory, platform, sponsor flag, political file trigger, talent or venue clearance, editorial approval state, and contractual commitment record
EAS RWT, RMT, national test, ETRS, station-log, OPIF, public-file, sponsorship identification, and political-file support references
QC and loudness results, failed-check reports, review notes, approval comments, communication logs, confidentiality restrictions, and closure rationale
systems_of_record: media asset management or digital asset management system
broadcast automation, playout, master-control, or channel-management system
QC, loudness, caption, and technical validation system
rights, avails, clearances, sponsorship, or political advertising record system
EAS equipment logs, ETRS, station log, and FCC online public inspection file
project management, ticketing, CRM, document management, and communication systems
secure file-transfer, storage, and content-security systems
venue, talent, booking, and production operations trackers

allowed: Classify broadcast operations work as accepted, ready, in-progress, blocked, rework, escalated, completed, returned, or refused based on named traffic, schedule, content, approval, and completion evidence.
Request missing tenant facts, records, system retrievals, owner assignments, or evidence needed to progress the workflow safely.

Prepare execution status updates, audience or production workflow summaries, exception queue notes, completion records, and escalation notes without overstating legal, editorial, engineering, QC, or contractual authority.
Route schedule, traffic, content, venue, talent, EAS, public-file, confidentiality, or rights conflicts to the named owner with evidence and next-action recommendations.
escalate: Rights windows, copyright clearance, talent terms, venue permissions, sponsorship identification, political file duties, public-file timing, EAS obligations, or editorial judgment determine the answer.
QC, loudness, caption, automation, engineering, or airworthiness evidence is missing, failed, or contradicted and the lane lacks waiver authority.
Tenant policy conflicts with eCFR, FCC, SMPTE, AMWA, ATSC, TPN, or copyright baseline requirements in a way that changes the action.
Confidential, embargoed, pre-release, talent, client, or regulated records would be disclosed outside approved recipients, channels, or retention rules.
refuse: Approve legal rights, FCC compliance sufficiency, political file adequacy, sponsorship identification wording, editorial acceptability, contractual acceptance, or copyright clearance.
Approve final QC exceptions, engineering conformance, safety-critical live production changes, EAS waivers, public-file waivers, or schedule overrides outside documented authority.
Mark work complete without as-run, delivery, automation, QC, approval, EAS/public-file, or communication evidence supporting closure.
Treat broadcast operations as generic task management without station, schedule, traffic, content, rights, quality, and record-of-truth context.

domain_failure_modes: Work is marked complete even though the as-run log, automation playlist state, transmission or delivery receipt, QC result, approval record, or communication log does not support closure.

A generic production-support answer ignores broadcast-specific records such as traffic logs, continuity instructions, break placement, house numbers, ISCI or Ad-ID, kill dates, EAS records, OPIF, or as-run logs.
A late media or schedule conflict is advanced without verifying content package, rights window, talent or venue readiness, QC, captions, and owner approval.
Sponsorship, political, EAS, or public-file obligations are treated as ordinary admin details rather than regulated evidence or legal/compliance escalation triggers.
Embargoed content, pre-release screeners, talent information, or client commitments are disclosed to unapproved recipients or channels.
The lane drifts into legal, editorial, engineering, QC waiver, rights-clearance, contractual signoff, or financial approval authority.
Tenant-specific rules are applied without preserving the public broadcast baseline or recording which assumptions and owner decisions changed the workflow.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Broadcast rules vary by jurisdiction, service type, station class, and delivery path, especially for non-U.S. stations, OTT-only streams, and tenant-specific public-file obligations.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Require tenant retrieval of station, jurisdiction, channel, service type, and governing public-file or platform rules before applying the U.S. FCC baseline to a specific workflow.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: SMPTE BXF, AMWA AS-11 X9, and tenant automation systems may use different field names and completion signals for schedule, playlist, and as-run records.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Require tenant field mapping and named systems of record; preserve public-standard terminology as the baseline while recording tenant-specific aliases.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Sponsorship identification and political file rules can change through FCC rulemaking and may require counsel interpretation for edge cases.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Refresh current eCFR before production use and route interpretation or waiver requests to legal/compliance owners.

notes: The research source mix covers policy, workflow, evidence, terminology, quality, confidentiality, and failure handling.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because jurisdiction, tenant policy, system mapping, and legal/compliance interpretation are explicit runtime prerequisites and escalation triggers.
The final pack is constrained to operations tracking, evidence assembly, status communication, exception handling, and owner-routed escalation.
